threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still queue for the bus without checking the TfL update app. Locals already know it's late and take their chances with Uber.
Last Friday on the 35, a guy serenaded us with a ukulele, then offered everyone a free slice of his homemade cake. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids trading Pokémon cards by the kanaal while an old man fed pigeons with one hand and sipped his coffee with the other. Pure Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a club in an old power plant, where techno beats mix with the hum of industrial machines. It's eerie and electrifying.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they know which taco truck to hit after a night out, not just where to find overpriced burritos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 Fulton today and the driver had a full-on chat with a rider about his cat's birthday. Only in SF do we celebrate the pets like family.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all tapas are the same, but locals know that a good patatas bravas varies wildly from bar to bar, and that's half the fun.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they keep pulling out their smartphones at every step to navigate, like it’s the first time they’ve seen the Plaça del Sol.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to the nearest Tube station like it's a shortcut, oblivious to the bumpy bus routes and that cycling is basically a rite of passage.
Caught the 198 last night, driver stopped for a couple arguing outside the bus. Ended up playing therapist. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I stumbled upon an open-air flea market at Maybachufer, where the aroma of grilled corn mixed with distant techno beats—that’s when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a park where the public lawn doubles as an open-air gym, complete with makeshift weights and yoga classes at dawn.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can name the local taco trucks but still get baffled by Muni routes after a rainy week.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy with a saxophone play an entire set at the bus stop. Muni was late, but everyone stayed to listen. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the best tapas are in fancy restaurants; the real gems are in the tiny bars where the locals squeeze in for a quick caña.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by their constant map-checking at every corner, like they’re searching for lost treasure in plain sight.
